pain_points: Limited multi-location reporting makes consolidating financials across properties time-consuming
No native hospitality chart of accounts requires significant customization
Manual data entry from POS and PMS creates reconciliation errors
Lacks food cost and recipe-level reporting needed for F&B operations
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable integrates directly with QuickBooks to automate journal entries from purchasing and inventory data, eliminating manual reconciliation and providing hospitality-specific reporting.

pain_points: Complex multi-entity setup requires expensive consulting to configure for hospitality
F&B procurement data must be manually mapped to GL codes
Month-end close delayed by missing invoice data from paper-based purchasing
Limited operational-level cost controls visible to kitchen and bar managers
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable's bi-directional Sage Intacct integration pushes coded AP transactions automatically, cutting close cycles and giving operators real-time cost visibility.

pain_points: High total cost of ownership with per-module licensing escalates as operations grow
Hospitality procurement workflows require custom SuiteScripts that are costly to maintain
Real-time inventory costing across outlets is complex without additional modules
Receiving and three-way matching breaks down for high-volume F&B invoices
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable layers hospitality-native procurement and inventory on top of NetSuite, replacing costly custom scripts with a purpose-built integration that keeps the ERP as the system of record.

pain_points: Sales data from Toast must be manually reconciled with purchasing costs to get true margin
No link between what was sold and what was ordered or received
Variance between theoretical and actual food cost is invisible without third-party tools
Menu engineering decisions made without real-time ingredient cost data
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable ingests Toast sales mix and maps it to recipe costing, surfacing actual vs. theoretical food cost variance daily so operators can take corrective action.

pain_points: Legacy MICROS systems produce sales data in formats incompatible with modern analytics tools
Recipe costing module is expensive and rarely kept up to date by operators
Void and comp tracking difficult to reconcile against purchasing records
Multi-outlet MICROS deployments create data silos per location
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable's MICROS connector normalizes sales data across versions and outlets, linking it to purchasing and inventory so every outlet's P&L reflects true operational cost.

pain_points: Aloha's back-office reporting requires manual export and reformatting for finance teams
No automated food cost reporting without additional NCR modules
Tip and labor data lives separately from food cost making holistic P&L difficult
Multi-unit operators struggle to aggregate Aloha data across sites
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable connects to Aloha to automate daily sales-to-cost reconciliation, removing the manual export burden and delivering multi-unit P&L in one dashboard.

pain_points: Outlet revenue data in Opera is not connected to F&B procurement costs
Banquet and catering orders require separate systems to cost accurately
Month-end accruals for F&B are manual estimates rather than actual purchasing data
Housekeeping and F&B supply consumption tracked separately with no unified view
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable integrates with Opera to connect outlet revenue to purchasing cost, automating F&B accruals and giving hotel controllers accurate departmental P&Ls.

pain_points: Mews's open marketplace integrations do not include a native procurement or inventory solution
F&B revenue recorded in Mews is not matched against procurement spend
Departmental cost reporting requires manual assembly from multiple systems
Growing hotel groups on Mews lack a scalable purchasing workflow
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable fills the procurement gap in the Mews ecosystem, connecting outlet revenue to purchasing data and scaling procurement workflows as properties are added.

pain_points: Cloudbeds lacks a built-in purchasing or inventory module for F&B operations
Controllers manually reconcile F&B costs against Cloudbeds revenue reports
No automated cost-per-occupied-room metric for supplies and F&B
Growing boutique hotel groups outgrow manual purchasing processes quickly
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable adds a complete procurement and inventory layer to Cloudbeds, automating purchasing approvals and syncing actuals to Cloudbeds revenue for true departmental margins.

pain_points: Lightspeed's reporting is strong on sales but has no connection to supplier invoices
Food cost percentage is calculated manually from periodic inventory counts
Multi-location Lightspeed users cannot compare cost performance across outlets
Purchasing approvals and order guides are managed outside the POS in spreadsheets
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable connects purchasing and inventory to Lightspeed sales data, automating food cost calculation and replacing spreadsheet order guides with digital purchase orders.

pain_points: Spreadsheet-based purchasing is error-prone and has no audit trail
Recipe costs are static snapshots that don't update when supplier prices change
Consolidating multiple outlet spreadsheets takes days of manual effort
No approval workflow means unauthorized purchases go undetected
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable replaces spreadsheet purchasing with a digital workflow including approval routing, supplier price syncing, and automatic cost updates when ingredients change.

pain_points: Coupa is designed for indirect spend and lacks hospitality-specific catalog and recipe costing
High implementation cost and complexity for F&B-heavy operations
Supplier onboarding in Coupa is slow for the fragmented hospitality supplier market
Recipe and menu engineering workflows are outside Coupa's scope
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable complements or replaces Coupa for hospitality F&B spend, offering purpose-built order guides, recipe costing, and supplier integrations designed for the restaurant and hotel industry.

pain_points: Birch Street's interface is outdated and requires significant staff training
Mobile receiving workflows are clunky, leading to workarounds and data gaps
Integration with modern cloud-based PMS and accounting systems is limited
Reporting customization requires vendor professional services
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable provides a modern, mobile-first alternative to Birch Street with intuitive receiving workflows, real-time cost visibility, and native integrations to cloud accounting and PMS platforms.

pain_points: MarketMan's ordering module requires manual price updates when supplier catalogs change
Limited accounting integration means AP staff still re-key invoice data
Multi-location inventory reconciliation is cumbersome with separate databases per site
Waste tracking relies on manual logging rather than automated variance detection

pain_points: Legacy platform with limited API access for modern integration needs
Labor and inventory data do not connect for prime cost reporting
Mobile inventory counting experience is dated compared to newer tools
Customer support and product development slower than cloud-native competitors
systems.craftable_angle: Craftable provides a modern cloud-native inventory and procurement platform with real-time supplier integrations and an intuitive mobile experience purpose-built for hospitality.

pain_points: Restaurant365 pricing is high for smaller or mixed hospitality operators
Implementation timeline is lengthy, delaying ROI for growing groups
Hotel F&B outlets have needs beyond R365's restaurant-centric design

pain_points: Compeat is a legacy platform with end-of-life risks as R365 consolidates the product
Integration with newer cloud POS and PMS platforms is limited

pain_points: Xero lacks hospitality-specific reporting and chart of accounts templates

pain_points: Revel's inventory module requires frequent manual updates to stay accurate

pain_points: Square lacks any procurement or inventory management for F&B operations
